_schema: Per-move YouTube upload metadata. Title <= 100 chars. Description plain-text, UTF-8.
_source: Bico Duro's beginner (cqKMvYbB1Kw) + intermediate rolê-drill (zLPVWP5WQOg) compilation videos. Sliced via segmentation_plan.json.
_lineage_attribution: — Bico Duro, Tribo Bahia Mirim, Baia Itacaré (Bahia, Brazil)

ginga.title: Ginga — foundational rocking step | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
ginga.transcript_pt: Vamos aprender a técnica de como se faz uma boa capoeira, com o passo básico para iniciante, ok? Ginga!
ginga.transcript_en: Let's learn the technique of how to do good capoeira, with the basic step for beginners, ok? Ginga!
ginga.summary_en: The Ginga is the rocking foundation of capoeira — every move begins and ends here. Bico Duro opens the beginner curriculum with it because everything else builds on this rhythm.

esquiva_lateral.title: Esquiva lateral — side dodge | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
esquiva_lateral.transcript_pt: Vamos fazer agora a defesa, pois a defesa é a segunda coisa mais importante dentro da capoeira. Esquiva lateral.
esquiva_lateral.transcript_en: Now we'll do defense, because defense is the second most important thing within capoeira. Side dodge.
esquiva_lateral.summary_en: The first defensive move in Bico Duro's curriculum. A lateral evasion that slips an incoming kick to the side while keeping the body low and ready to flow back into Ginga.

defesa.title: Defesa — basic block | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
defesa.summary_en: A standing block / cover. The simplest defensive shape — arms and body shielding center line. Often chained into other esquivas.

cocorinha_negativa.title: Cocorinha Negativa — low evasion + negativa | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
cocorinha_negativa.summary_en: Cocorinha (squatting low) into negativa (one leg extended, body close to the ground). Two of capoeira's signature ground-evasion shapes, used to slip under high kicks.

esquiva_para_tras.title: Esquiva para trás — backward dodge | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
esquiva_para_tras.summary_en: Evasion backwards — body weight shifts onto the back leg, torso leans away, hands stay up. Used against straight front kicks (bênção, ponteira) and chest-line attacks.

queda_de_quatro.title: Queda de quatro — four-point drop | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
queda_de_quatro.summary_en: Dropping into a four-point base — both hands and both feet on the ground. From here you can transition into kicks, flips, or evasions. A staple shape in the floor game.

bencao.title: Bênção — blessing front-thrust kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
bencao.transcript_pt: Vamos fazer agora a terceira coisa mais importante dentro da capoeira: os ataques, os golpes. Primeiramente, os golpes de linha. Linha de frente: bênção.
bencao.transcript_en: Now we'll do the third most important thing within capoeira: attacks — strikes. First, the line strikes. Front line: bênção.
bencao.summary_en: Bênção ("blessing") — a straight front-thrust kick delivered with the heel, hip behind the strike. The opening attack in Bico Duro's line-strike sequence.

martelo.title: Martelo — hammer kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
martelo.summary_en: Martelo — a hammer kick that whips in from the side, instep striking the head or body. Hip rotation drives the power; the support foot pivots a full quarter turn.

meia_lua_de_frente.title: Meia lua de frente — front half-moon kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
meia_lua_de_frente.summary_en: A half-moon arc kick traveling inward — from the outside line, sweeping across the opponent's centerline. The inside foot strikes; the body opens through the hip.

queixada_de_frente.title: Queixada de frente — front jaw-kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
queixada_de_frente.summary_en: Queixada — "jaw" — an outward-arc kick that opens the body, the outer edge of the foot rising to strike the opponent's jaw or temple. Mirror image of the meia lua de frente.

gancho.title: Gancho — hook kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
gancho.summary_en: Gancho — a hooking kick where the heel curls back to strike. Sometimes called "esporal." Lower-line variant; targets the ribs or hip from the outside.

ponteira.title: Ponteira — toe-point thrust kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
ponteira.summary_en: Ponteira — a sharp front-thrust kick striking with the ball or toe of the foot. Lives between bênção (heel) and chute (instep) in the line-strike family.

joelhada.title: Joelhada — knee strike | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
joelhada.summary_en: Joelhada — a knee strike driven up from the ginga. Close-range; targets the chest, jaw, or thigh of the opponent.

joelhada_lateral.title: Joelhada lateral — side knee strike | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
joelhada_lateral.summary_en: Joelhada lateral — the knee strikes from the side, hip rotating to expose the side of the knee as the contact surface. Close-range counter to a body clinch.

chute.title: Chute — basic kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
chute.summary_en: Chute — the generic term for a kick. In Bico Duro's progression, this is the straight, no-frills kick before the rotational and aerial work begins.

giro.title: Giro — pivot spin | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
giro.summary_en: Giro — the basic pivot/turn. Practiced in isolation so that when it joins armada, meia lua de compasso, or rabo de arraia, the spin itself is already automatic.

armada_de_costas.title: Armada de costas — back-spinning hook kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
armada_de_costas.summary_en: Armada de costas — armada delivered with the back turned to the target, the kick whipping around as the body completes a full rotation. Power comes from the windup.

rabo_de_arraia.title: Rabo de arraia — stingray-tail spinning kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
rabo_de_arraia.summary_en: Rabo de arraia — "stingray tail." A spinning low-handstand kick: the hands plant, the body inverts, and one leg whips around like a tail. One of capoeira's iconic shapes.

giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.name_pt: Giro da meia lua de compasso
giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.title: Giro da Meia lua de compasso — compass half-moon spin | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.transcript_pt: Giro da meia lua de compasso.
giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.transcript_en: The spin entry to the half-moon compass kick.
giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.summary_en: Drilling the spin (giro) that sets up the meia lua de compasso. Isolate the rotation before adding the kick — the precision is in the foot placement before launch.

queixada_cavando.title: Queixada cavando — digging jaw-kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
queixada_cavando.summary_en: Queixada cavando — "digging queixada." A lower-trajectory variant of the queixada that scoops upward, often used to break through a held guard rather than over it.

meia_lua_de_compasso.title: Meia lua de compasso — compass half-moon kick | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
meia_lua_de_compasso.summary_en: Meia lua de compasso — the signature spinning kick of capoeira. One hand plants, body opens like a compass, the heel swings in a wide arc. Precision: the planted hand and the kicking leg form a straight line.

chapeu_de_couro.title: Chapéu de couro — leather-hat sweep | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
chapeu_de_couro.summary_en: Chapéu de couro — "leather hat." A low spinning sweep: one hand plants while the body rotates and a leg sweeps the opponent's stance from below. Bico Duro closes the beginner curriculum with this move + rolê.

role_intro_modalidade.title: Modalidade com rolê — intro to the rolê drill | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_intro_modalidade.transcript_en: We're going to drill each move chained with rolê.
role_intro_modalidade.summary_en: Bico Duro's intermediate drill: every defensive shape and every attack ends with rolê — the defensive roll back to ginga. The whole video below pairs each move with rolê so the transition becomes automatic.

role_defesa.title: Defesa com rolê — block + defensive roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_defesa.summary_en: The basic block ending in rolê — a low rolling transition back to base. The point: never end a defense standing still; always flow.

role_negativa.title: Negativa com rolê — low evasion + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_negativa.summary_en: Negativa (low extended-leg evasion) followed by rolê. The body never stops moving — drop low, roll, recover on the other side.

role_cocorinha.title: Cocorinha com rolê — squat evasion + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_cocorinha.summary_en: From the deep squat (cocorinha), one leg extends and the body rolls into rolê. Trains the squat→roll transition cleanly.

role_esquiva_para_tras.title: Esquiva para trás com rolê — backward dodge + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_esquiva_para_tras.summary_en: Backward esquiva chained into rolê. Use this against a straight front-line attack (bênção, ponteira): the body retreats, drops, and rolls out laterally.

role_esquiva_martelo.title: Esquiva para martelo com rolê — martelo dodge + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_esquiva_martelo.summary_en: Specific evasion against an incoming martelo (hammer kick), followed by rolê. The body slips inside the arc of the kick, then drops into the roll for distance.

role_martelo.title: Martelo com rolê — hammer kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_martelo.summary_en: Throw the martelo and exit into rolê. Trains the kick-recovery — landing already on the floor, ready to roll, instead of standing upright and exposed.

role_gancho.title: Gancho com rolê — hook kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_gancho.summary_en: Gancho exiting into rolê. The hook lands; weight shifts down, the body folds into the rolling escape.

role_chapa.title: Chapa com rolê — push kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_chapa.summary_en: Chapa — a side-thrust push kick driven from the hip, sole of the foot striking the opponent's centerline — chained into rolê. Distance management at the moment of contact.

role_meia_lua_de_frente.name_pt: Meia lua de frente com rolê
role_meia_lua_de_frente.title: Meia lua de frente com rolê — front half-moon + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_meia_lua_de_frente.transcript_pt: Meia lua de frente com rolê.
role_meia_lua_de_frente.summary_en: Meia lua de frente lands and the body immediately spirals down into rolê. Trains the kick to terminate low rather than upright.

role_queixada_de_frente.title: Queixada de frente com rolê — front jaw-kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_queixada_de_frente.summary_en: Queixada de frente chained into rolê. The outward-arc kick recovers downward into the roll rather than returning to standing — keeps you out of counter-range.

role_joelhada.title: Joelhada com rolê — knee strike + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_joelhada.summary_en: Joelhada into rolê. After a close-range knee, the body drops and rolls — you don't stay in the clinch range to absorb a counter.

role_joelhada_lateral.title: Joelhada lateral com rolê — side knee + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_joelhada_lateral.summary_en: Side knee chained into rolê. Same logic as joelhada: strike, drop, roll out — never stand in counter-range.

role_queixada_de_frente_2.title: Queixada de frente com rolê (variação) — front jaw-kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim
role_queixada_de_frente_2.summary_en: A variation of the queixada de frente + rolê — Bico Duro returns to the same move later in the drill with a different entry angle.

role_chute.title: Chute com rolê — basic kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_chute.summary_en: Straight kick exiting into rolê. The simplest version of the kick-roll loop — drilled until the down-and-out becomes reflex.

role_giro_da_armada.title: Giro da armada com rolê — armada spin + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim Capoeira
role_giro_da_armada.summary_en: Armada delivered with the spinning entry, exiting into rolê. The full rotation drives the kick; the rolê absorbs the momentum on the way down.

role_giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.name_pt: Giro da meia lua de compasso com rolê
role_giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.title: Giro da meia lua de compasso com rolê — compass kick + roll | Tribo Bahia Mirim
role_giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.transcript_pt: Giro da meia lua de compasso com rolê. Essa é uma boa técnica para fazer bananeira na ginga ou jogando.
role_giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.transcript_en: Spin-entry compass half-moon kick, into rolê. This is a good technique for setting up the bananeira (handstand) within the ginga or in the game itself.
role_giro_da_meia_lua_de_compasso.summary_en: The signature spinning kick chained into rolê, with a key teaching note from Bico Duro: this drill builds the body awareness needed to set up a bananeira (handstand) inside live play.
